detailed_analysis: All three scoped files are Layer-1 (Entities) modules per architecture.md §2/§4. They are small, cohesive, and exemplary against the project's own engineering limits (functions <= 40 lines, modules <= 300 lines, two-word naming, fail-fast at boundaries). identity.py (19 lines) defines validate_pair_id, which fails loud with a descriptive InvalidPairId and verifies both UUIDv4 version and canonical text form (str(parsed) == value rejects non-canonical casing/format) — a clean enforcement of invariant I-1. artifact_names.py (43 lines) defines a single pure, keyword-only resolve_artifact_name that validates precedence tokens (raises ValueError on an unknown source), ignores empty strings to avoid name="" routing, and fails closed via required_label — strong fail-fast hygiene. sync_types.py (69 lines) defines three dataclasses for per-poll observation; the one mutable field (CustomizationArtifactInfo.agentic_tools) correctly uses field(default_factory=dict), so there is NO C4 mutable-default defect. The TYPE_CHECKING-guarded import of SharedKeyedMapLayout avoids an import cycle while keeping the inward-only Dependency Rule intact. No CRITICAL, MAJOR, or WARNING code-quality findings. Only one trivial INFO-grade observation: an intentional but undocumented type asymmetry in resolve_artifact_name parameters (frontmatter_name/prior_name typed `object`, path_name/override_name typed `str | None`). The single material issue is a spec/governance gap, not a code defect: artifact_names.py is a real production module imported by five Layer-3 adapters but is entirely absent from architecture.md — missing from the §4 module map (which asserts a 19-module total and assigns every module a layer) and from the §9 traceability table (which asserts every module carries at least one requirement/US). This violates the document's own stated completeness invariants.

dependency_graph_summary.notes: identity.py and sync_types.py have zero intra-package inward dependencies (Layer-1 leaves). artifact_names.py also has zero intra-package dependencies. sync_types.py imports agentic_tool_spec.SharedKeyedMapLayout only under TYPE_CHECKING, so no runtime/import cycle exists. All three are imported only by equal-or-outer layers (identity by canonical/archive/state/discovery/portable_archive; artifact_names by *_io adapters; sync_types by discovery/adoption/sync), consistent with the inward-only Dependency Rule (architecture §3).

findings.evidence:
frontmatter_name: object = None,
path_name: str | None = None,
prior_name: object = None,
override_name: str | None = None,
findings.problem: Two name sources (frontmatter_name, prior_name) are typed `object` while the other two (path_name, override_name) are typed `str | None`, yet all four are funnelled through `str(value)` identically. The `object` typing is presumably intentional (callers may pass non-str frontmatter values), but the asymmetry is undocumented and weakens the contract: a caller cannot tell from the signature which arguments accept arbitrary objects by design and which are merely loosely typed. Trivial; no behavioural impact.

deviations.description: artifact_names.py is a production Layer-1 module imported by five Layer-3 adapters (rules_io, copilot_io, cursor_io, opencode_io, gemini_cli_io) but is entirely absent from architecture.md. §4's module map lists neither the module nor its line count and asserts a total of '19 modules, 2 852 lines' that excludes it; §9 asserts 'every requirement ... and every story ... has at least one module carrying it' and 'any future requirement that has no carrying module is a defect', yet the module carrying the artifact-name precedence policy (relevant to US-04 rename / name-field resolution) is not traced. The governance documents are stale with respect to the code.

verdict_summary: Three exemplary Layer-1 entity modules with zero code-quality defects (code_score 1.0); the only issue is a governance gap — artifact_names.py is undocumented in architecture.md §4 and §9.
